title: Endoscopic decomposition of elliptic Fargues-Scholze L-packets

thm:6.summary: Elliptic $\mathcal{E}$-stability of FS packets: for every elliptic $L$-parameter $\varphi$, every virtual character $f \in \Theta_\varphi$ in the span of the Fargues–Scholze packet $\Pi^{\mathrm{FS}}_\varphi$, and every $[\kappa] \subseteq \overline{S}_\varphi$, the component $f_{[\kappa]}$ is $\mathcal{E}_{\varphi,[\kappa]}$-stable. The structural input that makes the endoscopic decomposition possible.

prop:1.9.summary: Reduces $\mathcal{E}$-stability to the elliptic locus: for a combination of supercuspidal characters, if the restriction to elliptic elements is $\mathcal{E}$-stable then the whole character is $\mathcal{E}$-stable.

lem:2.4.summary: Eigenspace criterion: a function on stable orbital integrals lies in the $[s]$-isotypic part iff it transforms by the character $\zeta_{[s]}$ under the center $\overline{\mathbb{Q}}_\ell[c_{\widehat{G}^{\mathrm{ad}}}]$ of the Hecke/spectral algebra.

prop:2.6.summary: The restriction map $\mathrm{res}_\gamma: \Theta_\varphi \to \mathrm{Fun}([\mathrm{Orb}^{\mathrm{st}}_\gamma])$ is equivariant for the center $\overline{\mathbb{Q}}_\ell[c_{\widehat{G}^{\mathrm{ad}}}]$ of the spectral action — the bridge that lets the spectral grading on $\Theta_\varphi$ be read off on stable orbital integrals.

prop:2.8.summary: $z$-embedding reduction: passing to a $z$-extension $G_1 \to G$ with connected center, restriction induces an isomorphism $\Theta_{\varphi_1} \cong \Theta_\varphi$ compatible with the spectral action, reducing Theorem 6 to the connected-center case.

lem:3.5.summary: For basic $b$, the canonical map $i_{b\natural}(A) \to i_{b*}(A)$ is an isomorphism, and the Hecke action preserves the subcategory $i_{b\natural}(D^{C_\varphi}(G_b(F))) \subseteq D^{C_\varphi}(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$.

lem:3.9.summary: The Fargues–Scholze spectral action of $\mathrm{Perf}([*/S_\varphi])$ on $D^\varphi(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$ preserves compact objects and the subcategory generated by the trivial stratum — the categorical mechanism transporting spectral structure to the level of characters.

lem:3.11.summary: Natural isomorphism $K_0(D^\varphi(G(F))^\omega) \cong \Theta_\varphi$, identifying the Grothendieck group of compact FS sheaves on the basic stratum with the span of packet characters — the dictionary between the categorical and character pictures.

claim:5.3.summary: Hecke compatibility under $z$-embedding: for the projection $\eta: \mathrm{Bun}_{G_1} \to \mathrm{Bun}_G$, $T_V(\eta^*A) \cong \eta^* T_{\widehat{\eta}^*V}(A)$, proved by repeating the Fargues–Scholze argument (their Theorem IX.6.1) almost verbatim. Supplies the spectral-action compatibility used in the $z$-embedding reduction.

prop:6.2.summary: Elliptic endoscopic decomposition: $\Theta_{\mathrm{ell}}(G(F)) = \bigoplus_{\mathcal{E}} \Theta_{\mathcal{E}}(G(F))$ over elliptic endoscopic triples, and $\mathcal{E}$-stability of the elliptic restriction lifts to $\mathcal{E}$-stability of the full elliptic character.

lem:6.3.summary: The subspaces of $\mathcal{E}$-stable invariant functions on elliptic elements, indexed by elliptic endoscopic triples, are linearly independent — gives the directness of the elliptic decomposition.

prop:A.4.summary: Levi induction decomposition: $\Theta(G(F)) = \bigoplus_M \Theta_{M\text{-}\mathrm{ell}}(G(F))$ over conjugacy classes of Levi subgroups, with $\iota_M^G$ inducing $\Theta_{\mathrm{ell}}(M(F))^{W(M)} \cong \Theta_{M\text{-}\mathrm{ell}}(G(F))$.

prop:A.5.summary: Refines the $M$-elliptic part into endoscopic pieces: $\Theta_{M\text{-}\mathrm{ell}}(G(F)) = \bigoplus_{\mathcal{E} \in \Psi_{\mathrm{ell}}(M)/W(M)} \Theta_{\mathcal{E}_G}(G(F))$.

thm:A.6.summary: Full endoscopic decomposition over $\mathbb{C}$: $\Theta(G(F)) = \bigoplus_{\mathcal{E} \in \Psi(G)} \Theta_{\mathcal{E}}(G(F))$. Assembles the Levi-induction and elliptic-endoscopic pieces into a global decomposition of the character space.

prop:A.10.summary: Levi compatibility of stable distributions: $\iota_M^G(\widehat{C}_{\mathcal{E}\text{-}\mathrm{st}}(M(F))) \subseteq \widehat{C}_{\mathcal{E}_G\text{-}\mathrm{st}}(G(F))$, generalizing the stable case of earlier Kazhdan–Varshavsky work to the endoscopic setting.

thm:B.4.summary: Endoscopic decomposition over an arbitrary coefficient field $K$: $\Theta_K(G(F)) = \bigoplus_{\mathcal{E} \in \Psi(G)} \Theta_{\mathcal{E},K}(G(F))$, extending Theorem A.6 beyond $\mathbb{C}$ by Galois-descent from the complex case.

lem:B.5.summary: Galois-descent input: for an algebraically closed $K \subseteq \mathbb{C}$, $\Theta_K(G(F)) = \Theta_{\mathbb{C}}(G(F))^{\mathrm{Gal}(\mathbb{C}/K)}$, and representations not defined over $K$ have infinite Galois orbit.

edges.note: Step 1 of the proof deduces $\mathcal{E}_{\varphi,[\kappa]}$-stability of the elliptic part from the spectral-equivariance of the restriction map (Prop 2.6).
The non-connected-center case is reduced to connected center via the $z$-embedding isomorphism $\Theta_{\varphi_1} \cong \Theta_\varphi$ (Prop 2.8).
Reduction of $\mathcal{E}$-stability to the elliptic locus (Prop 1.9) is the framework in which $f_{[\kappa]}$-stability is established.
Equivariance is checked through the $[s]$-eigenspace criterion of Lemma 2.4.
The restriction map's source $\Theta_\varphi$ is identified with $K_0$ of compact FS sheaves (Lemma 3.11), carrying the spectral action that makes $\mathrm{res}_\gamma$ equivariant.
The $K_0$ identification uses that the spectral action preserves compact objects (Lemma 3.9).
Preservation of the basic-stratum subcategory under Hecke/spectral action relies on the $i_{b\natural} \cong i_{b*}$ isomorphism (Lemma 3.5).
The $z$-embedding isomorphism is spectral-action-compatible because of the Hecke compatibility $T_V(\eta^*A) \cong \eta^* T_{\widehat{\eta}^*V}(A)$ (Claim 5.3).
The elliptic endoscopic decomposition is built from the $\mathcal{E}$-stability of FS packet components (Theorem 6).
Directness of the elliptic decomposition uses linear independence of the $\mathcal{E}$-stable subspaces (Lemma 6.3).
The $M$-elliptic endoscopic refinement is the elliptic decomposition (Prop 6.2) applied on each Levi $M$.
Compatibility of the pieces along Levi induction uses the stable-distribution inclusion (Prop A.10).
Global decomposition combines the Levi-induction decomposition (Prop A.4).
...with the endoscopic refinement of each $M$-elliptic piece (Prop A.5).
The arbitrary-coefficient-field decomposition is obtained from the complex case (Theorem A.6) by Galois descent.

spectral_action.description: The Fargues–Scholze spectral action on $D(\mathrm{Bun}_G)$ and its identification with the character space, imported as the engine.

estability.description: $\mathcal{E}$-stability of FS $L$-packet components, reduced to the connected-center elliptic case.

elliptic_decomposition.description: Endoscopic decomposition on the elliptic locus.

global_decomposition.description: Assembly into the full endoscopic decomposition over $\mathbb{C}$ and over arbitrary coefficient fields.

FS.title: Geometrization of the local Langlands correspondence
FS.role: the construction whose $L$-packets $\Pi^{\mathrm{FS}}$, Hecke functors, and spectral action are taken as input; Theorem IX.6.1 is repeated verbatim in §5

HJ.title: Hansen–Johansson (FS-packet lifting to the spectral category)
HJ.role: lift of FS packet members to $D^\varphi(G(F))$ used in Lemma 3.11

MW.title: Moeglin–Waldspurger, on Arthur's elliptic tempered characters
MW.role: Arthur's theorem on elliptic tempered characters used in Prop 6.2

KV2.title: Kazhdan–Varshavsky, earlier work on stable distributions
KV2.role: the stable-case Corollary 6.13 generalized in Prop A.10

KSV.title: Kazhdan–Schneider–Varshavsky, fields of definition of representations
KSV.role: field-of-definition results used in Lemma B.5
